title: UI: Pre-warm progress badge in #app-status
description: Show 'Indexing... N/M tracks' badge in the existing #app-status row while pre-warm runs. Hide on done.
acceptanceCriteria: Polls /api/warmup every 2 seconds while step != 'done'; stops polling once done.
Badge hides on done with a 500ms fade-out (existing app-status CSS pattern).
Verified in browser using playwright (per skill rule: include 'Verify in browser using playwright' for any UI change).
Vitest test for the polling helper covers start/stop/error-tolerance.

steps.details: docs/index.html: inside #app-status, add Indexing... 0 / 0. CSS: .hidden { display: none; } - already exists.
Add _pollWarmup() function: setInterval every 2000ms, fetch /api/warmup, if step === 'done' clearInterval + hide badge with .classList.remove('visible'); else update text + ensure visible.
On loadTracksFromServer() success in local mode, call _pollWarmup() once. The interval handle stored on a module-level var so it can be cleared.
Fetch errors during polling: silently ignore (network blip); next tick retries. Don't toast.
tests/web/ui-logic.test.js: extract _pollWarmup to module scope; stub fetch + setInterval; assert badge text updates, hides on done, retries on fetch error.

tests/e2e/qa-smoke.spec.ts: launch the app against a mocked /api/warmup endpoint that returns step='cache', done=1000, total=10000 for 2s then step='done'. Assert the badge appears, updates, and disappears.

technicalNotes: Polling cadence: 2s is the sweet spot - the user wants to see progress but not wake the network up too much.
Don't compete with the existing 60s setInterval that updates last-scan age in #app-status - separate setInterval handles.
